title: Fever and Rash - Kawasaki Disease
chief_complaint: Fever >39°C for 6 days unresponsive to antibiotics, bilateral conjunctivitis, strawberry tongue, rash on trunk, swollen hands and feet, and cervical lymphadenopathy.

physical_exam.mouth: Strawberry tongue, cracked red lips, pharyngeal erythema
physical_exam.skin: Polymorphous rash on trunk, erythema and edema of hands and feet

correct_diagnosis: Kawasaki Disease (Complete, with Coronary Artery Dilation)
key_learning_points: IVIG within 10 days prevents coronary aneurysms
Echo at diagnosis, 2 weeks, and 6-8 weeks

patient_responses.fever: The fever won't go down with Tylenol or antibiotics
patient_responses.eyes: His eyes are red but no discharge

feedback.incorrect_message: This is Kawasaki disease. The prolonged fever + 4 criteria + thrombocytosis confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Kawasaki: IVIG 2g/kg over 12h + high-dose aspirin (80-100mg/kg/day) until afebrile, then low-dose. Echo now, at 2 weeks, and 6-8 weeks. Treat within 10 days to prevent coronary aneurysms. Well done catching this early.
